strengths: 20+ years software engineering with proven distributed-systems expertise (Dunnhumby: microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O, real-time data)
Recent exposure to agentic AI infrastructure (MCP code generators, developer tooling at re:cinq) and founder of open-source event-driven platform
Demonstrated technical breadth across full stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Docker, CI/CD, AWS CDK, MongoDB, GraphQL) with ability to select right tools
concerns: No demonstrated technical leadership experience; all roles are individual contributor focused with no evidence of hiring, mentoring, or scaling teams from 10→30+ people
Minimal agentic AI production depth (~1-2 months at current role); lacks LLM selection expertise, fine-tuning experience, evaluation methodology, and production failure-mode intuition
Zero fintech domain knowledge; no experience with credit systems, payment flows, trading infrastructure, compliance frameworks, or operating AI in regulated environments
response.justification: Szabo is a capable full-stack engineer with strong distributed-systems credentials from Dunnhumby's massive-scale infrastructure work, providing a solid technical foundation. However, he lacks three critical CTO requirements: demonstrated technical leadership (no team management, hiring, or org scaling experience), substantive agentic-AI production knowledge (only ~2 months in current role with no LLM/evaluation background), and fintech domain expertise (credit risk, compliance, regulatory infrastructure). This role requires an executive capable of architecting multi-agent AI systems for regulated fintech while building and scaling engineering leadership. Szabo's profile fits a strong senior staff engineer, not a CTO-level leader for an AI-first fintech venture.
